channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Data Breach Confirmed After Australian Energy Giant Origin Is Hacked
item.description: A hacker claims to have stolen the information of 2 million Origin Energy customers and is threatening to leak it. The post Data Breach Confirmed After Australian Energy Giant Origin Is Hacked appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: [AINews] Black Forest Labs FLUX 3 - Multimodal Flow Models that beat Seedance 2.0, Gemini Omni and Grok Imagine, and FLUX-mimic video-action robotics model
item.description: A HUGE win for BFL!

item.title: AINTMA: Agentic AI Architecture for Autonomous Test Management with Generative Intelligence, Secure Cloud Communication and Adaptive Quality Analytics
item.description: arXiv:2607.20452v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern software quality assurance demands intelligent, autonomous systems capable of adaptive decision-making across distributed cloud environments. This paper presents AINTMA (Agentic Intelligent Test Management Architecture), a multi-agent agentic...

item.title: Marking the Wrong Symptoms: Evaluating LLM Watermarks in Medical Texts
item.description: arXiv:2607.20462v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into clinical workflows, stressing the need for reliable traceability of model-generated output with watermarking. Yet, most watermarks are evaluated on general-purpose benchmarks, leaving...

item.title: ClickGuard: Detecting and Spoiling Clickbait News with Informativeness Measures and Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.20463v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper presents an AI-driven browser extension that identifies clickbait to help users avoid misleading Internet articles. Moving beyond traditional detection, the application employs a hybrid machine learning architecture that combines...

item.title: Stochastic Sampling is Epistemically Shallow: The Dimensionality Gap Between Temperature Variation and Model Diversity in LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2607.20464v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When a language model gives different answers on repeated runs, does that variation reveal what it does not know? Self-consistency turns the variation into a per-question uncertainty estimate via majority voting. But does the same variation reveal...

item.title: JAXBench: Benchmarking Autonomous TPU Kernel Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2607.20466v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Rigorous benchmarks have driven progress in autonomous GPU kernel performance optimization by establishing a shared target to hillclimb on, but no equivalent exists for TPUs. We present JAXBench, a TPU-native benchmark suite for AI-generated kernel...

item.title: DataPrep-Bench: Benchmarking LLMs as Training Data Preparators
item.description: arXiv:2607.20465v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The quality of training data fundamentally determines the capabilities of large language models (LLMs), yet no unified benchmark exists to measure how well LLMs, agents, and data-centric workflows actually prepare training data end to end. We view...

item.title: PhantomFill: When the Form Demands an Answer, Language Models Invent One
item.description: arXiv:2607.20492v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Language models in production do not write prose. They fill forms: JSON fields, function arguments, extraction templates. We show that the form itself causes hallucination. We ask thirteen models the same question about the same input and change only...

item.title: The Active Ingredient in Muon's Grokking
item.description: arXiv:2607.20512v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Muon optimizer reaches the grokking threshold on modular arithmetic faster than AdamW. Prior work attributes this to "spectral-norm constraints plus orthogonalized momentum" but does not isolate which mechanism matters. To better understand Moun's...

item.title: Scaling Closed-Loop Feature Channel Configuration with LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2607.20516v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Promising initial results in closed-loop large-language-model-based channel-configuration search demonstrated that neural-network widths can be optimized directly through executable code generation and accuracy feedback. However, those results were...

item.title: Multimodal CoLRAG-TF: Triple-Filtered Retrieval for Complex PDFs
item.description: arXiv:2607.20517v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) over heterogeneous PDF collections remains challenging due to multimodal content, domain-specific terminology, and the need for multi-hop reasoning across dispersed evidence. We present Multimodal CoLRAG-TF, a...

item.title: What is Good? Extracting and Testing Implicit Theories of Literary Quality from LLM Reasoning Traces
item.description: arXiv:2607.20425v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: What makes writing "good" remains a persistent question in literary studies and computational linguistics. We present a two-study investigation of how reasoning-enabled LLMs evaluate literary quality. In Study 1, we construct a benchmark of 30 real...

item.title: Knowledge Injection Exists in MoE? Exploring Expert-Aware Contrast Decoding in MoE for Mitigating LLMs'Hallucinations
item.description: arXiv:2607.20426v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing LLM hallucination mitigation methods, including prompt engineering and model optimization, either hardly alter models'internal knowledge or have poor cross-domain generalization. Contrastive decoding mitigates hallucinations by using...

item.title: Is MoE Routing a Huffman Code? Discovering the Frequency-Diversity Law in Chain-of-Thought
item.description: arXiv:2607.20427v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts architectures have revolutionized scaling, yet the underlying logic of their routing remains a black box. In this paper, we uncover a fundamental governing principle: MoE routing is not merely selection, but a manifestation of...

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop Large Language Model Framework for Identification of Cutaneous Immune-Related Adverse Events
item.description: arXiv:2607.20428v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study evaluated a retrieval-augmented, multi-agent large language model (LLM)-driven, human-in-the-loop framework for detecting cutaneous immune-related adverse events (cirAEs) from clinical notes. Compared with unassisted manual review, the...

item.title: More Is Not More: What Matters for Diversity in LLM Opinions?
item.description: arXiv:2607.20429v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are increasingly used to simulate diverse human opinions in open-ended tasks such as synthetic surveys, focus group modeling, and public opinion prediction. However, LLM outputs exhibit systematic opinion homogenization....

item.title: AutoFlight Signs SF Express for Offshore eVTOL Operations, Accelerates Global Expansion With Indonesia Type Certification and Sea-to-Air Logistics Vision
item.description: AutoFlight partners with SF Express for offshore wind farm logistics using 2-ton eVTOL aircraft, achieves first overseas eVTOL type certificate in Indonesia, targets 300+ aircraft for marine energy market.

item.title: Surpassing Pi-Zero: Youibot FabriVLA 1B-Parameter Model Topping the Embodied Intelligence Leaderboard Marks the Shift From Parameter Competition to Architecture Competition
item.description: Youibot FabriVLA achieves 90.0% tier-average on Meta-World MT50 with just 1B parameters, surpassing Pi-Zero, as the industrial robot maker also debuts Fengxi humanoid robot with 4,000 unit orders.

item.title: Domestic Carbon Fiber Breaks the High-End Barrier: Sinotech 5,000-Ton Aerospace-Grade Capacity and T1100-Class Technology Reach International Frontier
item.description: Sinotech achieves T1100-grade carbon fiber breakthrough on both wet and dry-jet wet spinning routes, building 5,000-ton aerospace-grade capacity to challenge Toray dominance in high-end carbon fiber.

item.title: Computing Power Becomes the Lifeline: Tech Giants Scramble for AI Supernodes as Model Parameter Competitions Drive Unprecedented Infrastructure Demand
item.description: Huawei, ZTE, H3C, Sugon, and others race to build and deploy AI supernodes from 1024 to 100K GPU scale, as Kimi K3 and GLM-5 demand drives computing capacity to the breaking point.

item.title: CAS Space Lijian-1 Y15 Deploys 5 Satellites With Intelligent Computing Payloads as Monthly Launch Cadence Accelerates
item.description: CAS Space Lijian-1 rocket completes 15th flight with 110 satellites launched; payload includes MUSA GPU-powered AI computing satellite and first commercial space debris monitoring satellite.

item.title: Elevated API error rates and latency on gpt-image-2 model
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Images (Operational)

item.title: Some users may experience elevated error rates in ChatGPT
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational) Compliance API (Operational) Image Generation (Operational) File uploads (Operational) Deep Research (Operational) Codex in ChatGPT Desktop (Operational) Sites (Operational) Login...

item.title: Russian Espionage Group Exploited Zimbra Zero-Day to Steal Mail and 2FA Codes
item.description: A Russian state-supported espionage group spent months reading Western mailboxes through a then-unknown flaw in Zimbra's webmail client. The payload goes after the last 90 days of email, the organization's entire email directory, the password saved in the browser and the codes kept for two-factor...

item.title: Supercooled kidneys have been transplanted into pigs in a “landmark achievement”
item.description: When it comes to organ donation, time is everything. As soon as an organ has been carefully removed from a donor’s body, it starts to deteriorate. Surgeons have a matter of hours to get it into a recipient. Leave it too long and the organ will become unusable. In most cases, organs will be...

item.title: Elevated error rates
item.description: Status: IdentifiedWe have identified that users are experiencing elevated errors for the impacted services. We are working on implementing a mitigation with the downstream infrastructure provider.Affected components Batch (Operational) Fine-tuning (Operational) Realtime (Operational) CLI...

item.title: Issues connecting to resources in West US
item.description: Starting at 14:44 UTC on 23 July, 2026, customers may have experienced intermittent connectivity failures, increased latency, or difficulty accessing Azure services in West US region. Customers with traffic traversing the West US region may also experience downstream impact. Impacted services...

item.title: OpenAI Fixes ChatGPT Agent Flaw That Could Let Attackers Forge an AI Insider
item.description: AgentForger allows an attacker to create, insert and remotely control an invisible autonomous AI agent inside a victim organization. The post OpenAI Fixes ChatGPT Agent Flaw That Could Let Attackers Forge an AI Insider appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ThreatsDay: Android Spyware, PLC Attacks, AI Image Prompt Injection + 12 More Stories
item.description: Most of this week's trouble came dressed as something useful. A package stole data. A fake extension opened remote access. A safety app became spyware. An image gave hidden orders to an AI agent. Other threats hid in open systems, weak code, and normal network traffic. The threats change every...

item.title: Is Patching Dead? Vulnerability Management in the Post-Mythos Era
item.description: You cannot out-patch a machine that writes a working exploit from a vulnerability description in twenty hours. Stop trying to optimize a game you cannot win. The post Is Patching Dead? Vulnerability Management in the Post-Mythos Era appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Chick-fil-A Accounts Get Fried in Credential Stuffing Attack
item.description: Threat actors used credentials obtained from other companies to hack into Chick-fil-A One accounts. The post Chick-fil-A Accounts Get Fried in Credential Stuffing Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Abstract Raises $25 Million to Expand Composable Security Operations Platform
item.description: The latest investment round brings the total raised by Abstract to nearly $50 million. The post Abstract Raises $25 Million to Expand Composable Security Operations Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Claude Cowork Flaw Could Let AI Agent Escape Its VM and Access Mac Files
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a sandbox escape vulnerability in Anthropic's Claude Cowork that makes it possible to break out of the confines of a Linux virtual machine (VM) within which the agent runs to read or write files anywhere on the Mac. Accomplish AI, which shared details of the...

item.title: Chaos Ransomware Uses msaRAT to Route C2 Traffic Through Headless Chrome and Edge
item.description: The Chaos ransomware group ran its command-and-control through the victim's own browser. Cisco Talos on Thursday detailed msaRAT, the Rust implant behind it, found on a compromised Windows machine ahead of the encryptor. The implant never opens an outbound connection of its own. Its process talks...

item.title: China-Nexus JadeProx Uses New TriBack Loader in Government and Healthcare Attacks
item.description: An exposed Alibaba Cloud server has revealed a China-nexus operation that Group-IB tracks as JadeProx. The cluster has targeted government, healthcare, and education organizations across Asia and Latin America with a previously undocumented Windows loader called TriBack Loader. Group-IB found the...

item.title: The Download: energy transmission and US threats against Chinese AI
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The power line that could reshape New York’s grid is hitting snags During a heat wave on July 3, New York State’s grid imported enough electricity...

item.title: Challenge data missing in Analytics
item.description: Jul 23, 12:09 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 23, 11:44 UTC Identified - Cloudflare identified and is working on a resolution for Analytics data missing, specifically for Challenges. This issue started on 2026-07-22 15:10 UTC.We are working to mitigate this problem. More updates...

item.title: How AI helps scientists design the next generation of medicines
item.description: Designing and developing a new medicine is an expensive, failure-prone scientific challenge. A new drug can take many years to develop, at the cost of a significant investment. And even then, most possible candidates never reach the patient. For biologic medicines, therapies made from engineered...

item.title: UK and partners expose Russian state-supported actors for new ‘zero-click’ phishing campaign targeting Western organisations
item.description: GCHQ’s National Cyber Security Centre and international partners issue warning as ‘LAUNDRY BEAR’ cyber threat group exposed for targeted phishing campaign

item.title: Workers Analytics queries slowness
item.description: Jul 23, 10:23 UTC Investigating - Reporting and analytics for Cloudflare's Workers features is currently slow in returning results. These services are still fully operational, just analytics are delayed. We are working to mitigate this problem. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: Latency issues across a number of services
item.description: Jul 23, 09:39 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jul 23, 09:39 UTC Monitoring - The degradation has been mitigated. We are monitoring to...

item.title: The power line that could reshape New York’s grid is hitting snags
item.description: On July 3, as a heat wave swept the region, New York State’s grid imported 52 gigawatt-hours of electricity from Canada—enough to meet about 9% of its total electricity demand that day. Some of that power shuttled in on a 339-mile power line stretching from Quebec to Queens called the Champlain...

item.title: [AINews] "Laguna S 2.1 Released: Cheaper than Deepseek v4 Flash, Better than V4 Pro"
item.description: a quiet day lets us highlight a new neolab win.

item.title: Inside the Model Factory — Eiso Kant, Poolside AI
item.description: Poolside's co-CEO on how his small team of top researchers built a model factory capable of training Laguna S - a 118B MOE beating Thinky's ~1T open weights model... and this is just the beginning.

item.title: Elevated errors on Haiku 4.5
item.description: Jul 23, 00:10 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 22, 23:52 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Bringing Nunchaku 4-bit Diffusion Inference to Diffusers
Disruption with actions hosted runners
item.description: Jul 22, 20:47 UTC Update - Approximately 3% of GitHub Actions runs on GitHub-hosted runners are experiencing run start delays exceeding 5 minutes. A small portion of these runs may fail after extended delays. We have identified the cause and are working on a mitigation. Jul 22, 20:43 UTC...

item.title: Customers unable to create Access and Device Enrollment policies via the Dashboard and API
item.description: Jul 22, 19:38 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 22, 19:02 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 22, 18:35 UTC Identified - Cloudflare engineering is investigating an issue preventing customers from creating new Access and Device...

item.title: Microsoft Office add-in availability
item.description: Jul 22, 17:56 UTC Identified - Users who installed the following add-ins may experience issues accessing the add-in functionality: Claude by Anthropic for PowerPoint, Claude by Anthropic for Word, and Claude by Anthropic for Excel. To regain immediate access to Claude for Microsoft 365, install the...
